data.title: Solana Air Gap: Analyst Says No Major Support Level Until $24
data.description:
An analyst has pointed out how a sort of supply “air gap” exists for Solana below $144, with no major on-chain support levels until much lower.

Solana URPD Reveals Supply Chasm Below $144

In a new post on X, analyst Ali Martinez has talked about how Solana support is looking from an on-chain lens. In on-chain analysis, the potential of any price level to act as support or resistance lies in the amount of coins that investors last purchased at it.

The reasoning behind this is that holders look at their cost basis as a special level and are sensitive to retests of it. The more holders that have their cost basis at a particular level, the larger the reaction from a retest could theoretically be.

As for what the nature of this reaction is likely to be, it comes down to the direction of the retest, as well as the mood in the market. When the retest occurs from above, holders might decide to accumulate more, thinking that the decline is a temporary dip and they would return in profit again.

Retests of major supply levels from above can, for this reason, provide support to the cryptocurrency. Similarly, retests in the opposite direction may be met with resistance, as holders panic exit at their break-even level, fearing going underwater again.

To showcase how the supply cost basis distribution on the Solana network is like right now, Martinez has shared the data of the UTXO Realized Price Distribution (URPD) from Glassnode.

As is visible in the above graph, the largest Solana supply zones on this indicator are all located above $144. Below this level, the cryptocurrency has relatively thin clusters. “There’s barely any meaningful demand until $24,” noted the analyst.

SOL has already started slipping under the last major support level of $144, so it only remains to be seen how the cryptocurrency will develop in the near future, considering the lack of any meaningful on-chain support cushions.

In a separate X post, Martinez has also shared the URPD data for Bitcoin. Unlike Solana, the number one cryptocurrency’s supply distribution is more even, meaning the asset has levels to rely on below the current range.

In particular, $82,000 and $67,000 are two levels below $95,000 that hold the cost basis of a significant amount of supply, and thus, could potentially be support barriers on the way down.

SOL Price

Solana dropped to $135 during its latest plunge, but the coin has since recovered back to $141.

data.title: Key facts: Zcash Gains Traction; EU Rules Spark Privacy Demand; Winklevoss Launches ZEC Treasury
data.description:
• Zcash uses zero-knowledge proofs for private transactions, boosting its attractiveness compared to Bitcoin and Ethereum, and has seen significant inflows into the Grayscale Zcash Trust.1
  • Demand for Zcash (ZEC) is rising amid privacy concerns, especially after new EU rules impose strict limits on cash transactions and require identity checks for larger crypto trades.2
  • The Winklevoss twins launched Cypherpunk Technologies, a ZEC-based treasury company, after acquiring and rebranding Leap Therapeutics, highlighting Zcash's potential in the crypto market.3

data.title: Key facts: Tether leads stablecoin market; issues $1.5B in credit; partners on tokenized securities
data.description:
• Tether (USDTUSD) remains the top stablecoin, with forecasts suggesting it will earn much higher net income than Circle, potentially dozens of times more in 2025.1
  • Tether has issued around $1.5 billion in credit to commodity traders, using cash and its USDT stablecoin, targeting traditional trades like agriculture and oil.2
  • Tether has partnered with KraneShares and Bitfinex Securities to promote tokenized securities and is working with Da Nang, Vietnam, to improve public digital infrastructure.3

data.title: Key facts: Solana Faces Resistance at $190-$215; Stablecoin Integration Boosts User Experience; Investor Support Strengthens
data.description:
• A crypto analyst highlights a key resistance zone for Solana (SOL) at $190 to $215. Recent rejections suggest sellers dominate, raising concerns about potential price drops.1
  • Solana's stablecoin payment integration enhances wallet and DEX user experience, enabling cross-chain swaps and presale access, potentially benefiting projects like Best Wallet and its $BEST token.2
  • Solana has gained support from various investors, helping it stay strong amid a market sell-off due to Bitcoin's drop, as funds shift to promising altcoins like Solana.3

data.title: Key facts: Bitcoin Drops 1.9% to $96,919; Bear Market Declared
data.description:
• Bitcoin's price fell due to macro risks, selling by long-term holders, and lower institutional demand. Future movements depend on renewed institutional interest and liquidity.1
  • Bitcoin fell 1.9% to $96,919, after a six-month low of $96,025, due to rising risk aversion and reduced support from major funds and corporate treasuries.2
  • Bitcoin has entered a bear market, dropping over 20% in a month, with $870 million in redemptions from exchange-traded funds reported on Thursday.3

data.title: Key facts: Dogecoin dips 2.3% as 21Shares launches ETFs; Markus comments
data.description:
• Dogecoin (DOGEUSD) is trading at $0.165, down 2.3% this week and 8% in one day, amid a bearish trend in the crypto market due to economic uncertainty.1
  • 21Shares launched two ETFs featuring Dogecoin, enabling investors to access DOGE without the need for wallet management.2
  • Billy Markus, the creator of Dogecoin, addressed the recent decline in Bitcoin's price, which has resulted in a substantial loss exceeding $200 billion in market capitalization.3

data.title: Key facts: Canary Capital launches U.S. spot XRP ETF; $58M volume; XRP supply drops
data.description:
• On November 13, 2025, Canary Capital launched the first U.S. spot XRP ETF, XRPC, on Nasdaq, achieving a record $58 million trading volume on its first day.123
  • XRP supply on exchanges dropped sharply, with 149 million XRP worth about $336 million withdrawn in 24 hours, indicating major accumulation and tighter liquidity ahead of new ETF launches.4
  • The launch of the XRPC ETF is expected to boost awareness of XRP's utility, likely increasing adoption and liquidity as more institutions invest in regulated products.5

data.title: Key facts: BTCS Q3 2025 net income at $65.59M; Ethereum ETFs face $259M outflows
data.description:
• BTCS reported a Q3 2025 net income of $65.59 million, significantly exceeding analyst expectations, driven by a $73.72 million increase in the fair value of its crypto assets, primarily Ethereum.1
  • Ethereum ETFs experienced $259 million in outflows on Thursday, the largest since October 10, totaling $1.42 billion in net outflows since early November due to institutional selling.2
  • Recent Ethereum whale activity shows mixed strategies: some long-term holders sold 2,404 ETH for $7.7 million, while a super-whale added 16,937 ETH, totaling 422,175 ETH.3

data.title: Key facts: Aave Labs approved under MiCA for zero-fee conversions; BTCS borrows $56.5M from Aave
data.description:
• Aave Labs gained approval under Europe's MiCA regulation for its 'Push' service, allowing euro and crypto conversions, including GHO, with zero fees, reducing reliance on centralized exchanges.1
  • BTCS Inc. has integrated Aave for ETH-backed liquidity and yield, borrowing $56.5 million from Aave in Q3 to boost its Ethereum exposure.2

data.title: Trillion Energy Announces Debt Settlements
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2025) - Trillion Energy International Inc.  (OTCQB: TRLEF) (FSE: Z62) ("Trillion" or the "Company"), announces that it proposes to issue an aggregate of 3,572,376 common shares of the Company at $0.035 per share in settlement of $125,033.16 in debt owed by the Company to various consultants of the Company (the "Debt Settlement"). The common shares will be subject to a four month and one day hold period from the date of issuance as per applicable Canadian securities legislation.

About the Company

Trillion Energy International Inc is focused on oil and natural gas production for Europe and Türkiye with natural gas assets in Türkiye. The Company is 49% owner of the SASB natural gas field, a Black Sea natural gas development and a 19.6% (except three wells with 9.8%) interest in the Cendere oil field. More information may be found on www.sedarplus.ca, and our website.

Contact

Sean Stofer, Chairman

Brian Park, VP of Finance

1-778-819-1585

E-mail: info@trillionenergy.com

Website: www.trillionenergy.com

Cautionary Statement Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

This news release may contain certain forward-looking information and statements, including without limitation, statements pertaining to the Company's ability to obtain regulatory approval of the executive officer and director appointments. All statements included herein, other than statements of historical fact, are forward-looking information and such information involves various risks and uncertainties. Trillion does not undertake to update any forward-looking information except in accordance with applicable securities laws.

These statements are no guarantee of future performance and are subject to certain risks, uncertainties, delay, change of strategy, and assumptions that are difficult to predict and which may change over time. Accordingly, actual results and strategies could differ materially and adversely from those expressed in any forward-looking statements as a result of various factors. These factors include unforeseen securities regulatory challenges, COVID, oil and gas price fluctuations, operational and geological risks, changes in capital raising strategies, the ability of the Company to raise necessary funds for development; the outcome of commercial negotiations; changes in technical or operating conditions; the cost of extracting gas and oil may increase and be too costly so that it is uneconomic and not profitable to do so and other factors discussed from time to time in the Company's filings on www.sedarplus.ca, including the most recently filed Annual Report on Form 20-F and subsequent filings. For a full summary of our oil and gas reserves information for Turkey, please refer to our Forms F-1,2,3 51-101 filed on www.sedarplus.ca, and or request a copy of our reserves report effective December 31, 2024 and filed on April 25, 2025.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/274674

data.title: Walmart Picks Insider to Take Over as Next CEO — WSJ
data.description:
By Sarah Nassauer and Emily Glazer

Walmart picked one of its longtime executives, John Furner, to become its next chief executive officer, preserving its tradition of promoting an insider to steer the nation's biggest retailer.

Furner, 51 years old, was seen inside the company as the likely heir apparent and ready for his next step after six years of leading Walmart's U.S. business. Outside the company, the Walmart veteran — and son of a Walmart executive — was seen as a potential CEO candidate for other companies too.

Walmart directors and executives discussed as early as this summer that Furner would get the top job and also thought that longtime CEO Doug McMillon should stay on as long as possible, according to people familiar with the discussions.

Walmart's announcement that Furner will take over in February ensures that the board's preferred candidate will lead the massive company at a critical time as it navigates a complex consumer and government landscape. He will be the company's fifth CEO since the founder, Sam Walton.

McMillon, 59, successfully steered the retailer for over a decade, managing through the Covid pandemic, expanding its online operations and most recently navigating President Trump's tariffs. McMillon, who has spent 40 years at Walmart, will remain on Walmart's board through next spring and stay on as an adviser through 2027.

The handoff comes after McMillon opened a sprawling new headquarters campus earlier this year in the company's hometown of Bentonville, Ark., a project that took years to coalesce.

Furner thanked McMillon and praised his tenure in a letter to Walmart employees, but said he was also focused on his current role. "As we head into the holiday season, my priority is leading the U.S. segment to a strong finish," he said.

While Furner's selection wasn't surprising, it hadn't been clear when he would ascend to the CEO role. In 2023 the board and McMillon discussed a plan to have the CEO stay in the role for at least three more years, The Wall Street Journal reported at the time. Walmart typically announces significant executive changes a few months before its fiscal year ends in January and hands off roles after the holidays.

A Walmart spokesman, Dan Bartlett, said the leadership transition was planned. "Doug was ready to retire, and he and the board saw that another wave of change is happening in the business and retail world, primarily fueled by AI," he said. "They recognized that John is uniquely capable of leading us through this period of change."

Furner has served as CEO of Walmart U.S. since 2019, overseeing the company's largest operating segment and more than 4,600 stores. He grew up in Arkansas, and his first job with Walmart was a part-time role in a local store in 1993. He has held leadership roles across merchandising, operations and sourcing internationally, as well as at Walmart's warehouse chain, Sam's Club.

McMillon praised Furner's experience in a note to staff Friday and said his long history with the company and varied roles would help him lead "with the vision and experience to guide our people through a new retail era fueled by innovation and AI," he wrote.

McMillon has led Walmart through a period of fast growth as the retail company remade its operations to focus more on e-commerce sales in the face of competition from Amazon.com. Under McMillon's tenure, Walmart shares have risen more than 400% on a total-return basis, and the company has gained $576 billion in market capitalization, according to a Wall Street Journal analysis. It has also grown to more than $680 billion in annual revenue.

Walmart shares closed down slightly at $102.48 on Friday.

When McMillon took the CEO job in 2014, Walmart was struggling. Its sales were stagnant, and it had grown so big that its own executives doubted it could keep growing. Its workers were unhappy, and its stores messy.

McMillon remade Walmart's strategy, including a plan to raise hourly worker wages, invest more in stores and grow online to fend off Amazon. In 2016, he led Walmart's purchase of an e-commerce startup, Jet.com, for $3.3 billion and placed its founder, Marc Lore, in charge of U.S. e-commerce operations. While e-commerce sales took off, many of the e-commerce projects of that era were eventually closed, including Jet.com.

In recent years, McMillon has worked to diversify Walmart's sources of revenue by pushing into advertising and memberships. "There have been times when some people doubted us," McMillon said in his letter to staff Friday. "Those people underestimated how much you care, and they underestimated your ability to change."

McMillon and Furner have similar backgrounds, but different management styles, said current and former Walmart executives. Both attended the University of Arkansas and have deep connections to Walmart, its northwestern Arkansas headquarters region and the family of Sam Walton, who hold two board seats and own significant stock.

McMillon is known to have a gentle management touch and be somewhat conflict-averse, while Furner has a more direct style. Furner embraces new technology and ideas and sometimes ruffles feathers, according to people familiar with the situation.

Walmart faces a challenging landscape as Furner takes over. Its sales grew rapidly throughout the pandemic as shoppers scrambled for food and toilet paper, and then as inflation made its low-price groceries and home goods appealing to many shoppers. Walmart and Amazon have become behemoths vacuuming up market share and challenging competitors.

Now Walmart contends with an evolving landscape related to artificial intelligence and uncertain consumer demand. In recent months, Walmart has hired a head of AI acceleration, and McMillon has said the company is bracing for AI to "to change literally every job."

In a sign of how significant Walmart executives believe AI will be, the company recently announced a shopping partnership with OpenAI to let shoppers buy its products directly within ChatGPT.

A search bar has long been the way shoppers browse and buy online, said McMillon last month. "That is about to change," he said.

Write to Sarah Nassauer at Sarah.Nassauer@wsj.com and Emily Glazer at Emily.Glazer@wsj.com

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Bought Alphabet Shares Worth $4.3 Billion. Here's What It Sold in the Third Quarter. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Evie Liu

Berkshire Hathaway bought $4.3 billion worth of Alphabet shares in the third quarter, according to the company's latest 13-F report. The filing, released on Friday, detailed the firm's equity holdings of U.S.-listed securities as of Sept. 30.

Alphabet made up 1.6% of Berkshire's portfolio by the end of September. The firm didn't own any Alphabet shares as of the end of the second quarter.

Berkshire also bought holdings in insurance company Chubb valued at $1.2 billion, boosting its share in the portfolio to 3.3%.

In the third quarter, Berkshire sold $10.6 billion worth of Apple shares, $1.9 billion worth of Bank of America stock, and $1.2 billion worth of holdings of VeriSign, a provider of domain registry services, the report showed.

Berkshire has been reducing its stakes in Apple and Bank of America for some time. Its stake in Apple has dropped from about 900 million shares at year-end 2023 to 238 million by the end of September, and its Bank of America position has shrunk by 45% since July 2024.

Still, Apple remains Berkshire's largest holding; it comprises 22.7% of the portfolio. Bank of America is the third-largest holding, and comprises 11% of the portfolio

Berkshire didn't change the position of its second-largest holding, American Express, which made up 18.8% of the portfolio by the end of September.

Berkshire did not increase its holdings in health insurance and benefits company UnitedHealth Group in the third quarter. The company bought five million shares of UnitedHealth in the second quarter, which spurred a rally in the stock in mid-August. UnitedHealth comprised 0.65% of Berkshire's portfolio as of the end of September.

Write to Evie Liu at evie.liu@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Cattle futures rebound after falling on softer cash trade
data.description:
Cattle futures on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange rebounded on Friday after falling the previous day, driven by weakening cash cattle prices and concerns about the wider economy.

CME February live cattle futures (LCG25) settled up 0.575 cents at 219.525 cents per pound. January feeder cattle (FCF26) futures ended up 2.100 cents at 320.550 cents per pound.

Daily limits widened to 13.750 cents for feeder cattle futures and 10.750 cents for live cattle in Friday's trading session, the exchange said.

Cattle traded lightly in Kansas and Texas cash markets at $228 per hundredweight (cwt), traders said, down $2 to $4 from last week's cash trades.

Meanwhile, stock market dips this week also raised fears about consumer demand for beef.

"The question remains, are we in a cyclical bear market?" said Don Roose, president of U.S. Commodities.

"The market continually cannot hold rallies," Roose said.

Traders continue to monitor an outbreak of the New World screwworm parasite in Mexico. The U.S. Department of Agriculture announced the opening of a sterile fly dispersal facility in Tampico, Mexico, as part of efforts to eradicate the pest by reducing the population of wild parasitic flies.

CME December lean hog futures (LHG25) settled up 0.525 cent at 79.375 cents per pound.

The CME's Lean Hog Index , a two-day weighted average of cash prices, fell 30 cents to $89.13 per cwt.

Traders also assessed the release of the USDA's monthly crop supply/demand report , the first since mid-September after the 43-day government shutdown saw it suspended.

data.title: Saab signs $3.6 billion Gripen fighter deal with Colombia
data.description:
Swedish defense firm Saab said on Friday it has signed a contract worth 3.1 billion euros ($3.62 billion) with the Colombian government to deliver 17 Gripen fighter jets in the next five years.

Saab will deliver 15 Gripen E single-seat and two Gripen F dual-seat fighter jets to Colombia and support military and civilian projects under two offset agreements focused on defense, technology and infrastructure.

The Gripen is a fourth-generation, single-engine supersonic fighter jet built for versatility. Classified as a multi-role aircraft, it is capable of executing air-to-air combat, precision bombing and reconnaissance missions. The jet is widely regarded as a cost-effective alternative to pricier fifth-generation fighters like the F-35.

Colombia had a choice between the JAS 39 Gripen, French Dassault-made Rafale <AM.PA>, and U.S. firm Lockheed Martin's F-16 <LMT.N> jets.

Neighbor Brazil has already purchased 36 JAS 39 Gripen jets from SAAB and built an assembly plant for the aircraft in the country in partnership with SAAB.

($1 = 0.8575 euros)

data.title: Aberdeen International Names Woodhead as CFO
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Aberdeen International named Stephen Woodhead as chief financial officer, effective immediately.

Woodhead joins the mining investment company with more than three decades of experience in the resource industry and worked for the South African diamond producer Trans Hex Group.

He has also worked at Black Iron, Desert Sun Mining and Homeland Energy.

Siobhan Boylan has been serving as Aberdeen's finance chief since February 2025.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com
